expect: At n>200, lane concurrency positive-quality effect (r≈+0.3) holds; 6+ lane decline testable with sufficient data
actual: L-840 reversal is a METRIC CONFOUND. Raw r flips to -0.207 (n=180) because lesson count/session penalizes focused DOMEX work. When quality is measured by Sharpe: DOMEX Sh=8.2±1.2 (n=139) vs non-DOMEX Sh=7.8±1.3 (n=416), +4.9% premium. Structure STILL improves quality — the metric was wrong.
diff: Expected r≈+0.3 to hold at n>200. Got r=-0.207 at n=180 — but this is confound, not falsification. The correct quality metric (Sharpe) confirms L-840's core claim. 6+ lane decline untestable (n=1 session with 6+ lanes). Key methodological insight: count-based proxies break when session types diverge.
verdict: L-840 core finding SURVIVES with corrected metric. Count-based quality is confounded by session type. Sharpe shows +4.9% DOMEX premium. 6+ lane decline has insufficient data.
